title: network_switch_failure
description: A core network switch fails, causing widespread connectivity loss across a datacenter floor.

timeline.title: Connectivity Loss Reported
timeline.description: Multiple servers on the same network segment simultaneously lose connectivity. Monitoring shows mass-ping failures.

timeline.title: Switch Unresponsive
timeline.description: The core switch management interface is unreachable. Physical inspection shows no link lights on uplink ports.

timeline.title: Incident Declared
timeline.description: Network team declares a P1 incident and begins isolating the failed switch from the topology.

timeline.title: Spare Switch Located
timeline.description: A spare switch with identical configuration backup is located in the storage room.

timeline.title: Replacement Switch Installed
timeline.description: The spare switch is racked, powered on, and configuration is restored from backup.

timeline.title: Connectivity Restored
timeline.description: All servers regain network connectivity. Services resume normal operation.

node-1.title: Mass Connectivity Loss
node-1.situation: Monitoring alerts show 47 servers on Floor 2 have simultaneously lost network connectivity. The core switch for that segment is unresponsive. You are on-site and have physical access to the network closet.
node-1.question: What is your immediate action?

options.label: A
options.text: Physically inspect the switch for power and link-light status, attempt a console connection to diagnose the fault, and declare a P1 incident while notifying the network team.

options.label: B
options.text: Power-cycle the switch immediately without assessing whether doing so could cause additional issues.

options.label: C
options.text: Begin reconfiguring all 47 servers with new static routes to bypass the switch before diagnosing the fault.

node-2.title: Switch Hardware Fault Confirmed
node-2.situation: Console connection shows the switch powered on but the switching fabric is unresponsive. A hardware fault has caused the backplane to fail. Power-cycling does not restore operation. A spare switch with the same model is available with a configuration backup on file.
node-2.question: How do you proceed?

options.label: A
options.text: Begin physical replacement by racking the spare switch, restoring configuration from backup, and preparing to reconnect all uplinks in a controlled sequence.

options.label: B
options.text: Attempt to repair the switching fabric on the live failed unit while servers remain disconnected.

node-3.title: Replacement Switch Configuration
node-3.situation: The spare switch is racked and powered on. The configuration backup is 3 days old and may be missing some recent VLAN additions. Before reconnecting uplinks, you need to decide how to handle the potentially stale configuration.
node-3.question: What do you do?

options.label: A
options.text: Restore the 3-day-old configuration, reconnect uplinks, verify connectivity for all segments, and manually add any missing VLAN configurations identified from change logs.

options.label: B
options.text: Skip configuration restoration and manually configure the switch from scratch during the outage to ensure it is current.

node-4.title: Connectivity Validation
node-4.situation: The replacement switch is active and most servers have regained connectivity. Two VLANs are missing from the configuration. Change logs show these were added 2 days ago.
node-4.question: What is your next step?

options.label: A
options.text: Add the two missing VLANs from the change log, verify full connectivity for all servers, update the configuration backup immediately, and schedule a review of the switch backup process.

options.label: B
options.text: Leave the two missing VLANs and close the incident, expecting the teams that own those VLANs to notice and report separately.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: You successfully diagnosed the switch hardware failure, executed a controlled replacement using a spare unit, restored configuration from backup, applied missing changes from the change log, and identified a gap in the backup frequency process.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
